def: Maṛ. n. m. S. भृज् H. bhūnnā to fry. 1. Mashed vegetables which have been boiled or fried. Bhurte ko mārū baiṅgan haiṅ! Delhi streetcry. 2. A mash; squash. part. & s.m. Been, become, produced, formed, being, existing, existent; actually being, really happened, true; right, proper; past, gone, former; — a created being, child, son, youth; a great devotee or ascetic; a kind of demigod; a kind of malignant spirit (believed by the Hindūs to haunt graveyards, lurk in trees, animate carcases, and delude or devour human beings), evil spirit, demon, ghost, goblin, imp; a vicious or wicked person, a devil; an ugly or hideous-looking person; an epithet of Śiva; (in Gram.) the preterite tense; (in Philos.) an element (of which, according to the Hindūs, there are five, viz. earth, water, fire, air, ether); (in Law) the real state of the case, the matter of fact

n. m. 1. Middle; centre ( markaz ). 2. Average; mean ( ausat ). 3. Difference; interval. 4. Room; space; place. Til dharne ko bīch nahīṅ. bīch bichāo , n. m. 1. Mediation; intervention; interposition; mediatorship; intercession. 2. Settlement; reconciliation; pacification. bīch bichāo karnā , v. a. 1. To mediate; intercede; interpose. 2. To settle; adjust (a difference); reconcile; pacify. bīch kī rās , adj. Middling; passable. bīch ke ghaṛe kī , adj. The very best, strong wine. bīch meṅ paṛnā, yā ānā , v. n. See bīch meṅ ānā , under ānā . 1. To negotiate in a bargain or marriage. 2. To be surety or security for. Khiṅchā khiṅchā woh phire jo parāë bīch meṅ paṛe . Prov. Who interpose in others' cause are dragged about as each one draws. bīch meṅ denā , v. n. To give a pledge.

(see بهيتر) 1. adv. In, into, among, between, during. 2. s. m. Middle, midst, centre. 3. Difference. 4. Quarrel, hostility. بيچ بچاؤ bīch-bichāʼo, Mediation, interposition, arbitration. بيچ بچاؤکرنا bīch-bichāʼo karnā, To mediate, interpose. بيچ پڙنا bīch paṛnā, v. n. To differ, to raise a quarrel between. بيچ کي انگلي bīch kī unglī, The middle finger. بيچون بيچ bīchoṅ bīch, s. m. The very middle, the very midst.
subs: bīćam-bīć, bīćoṅ-bīć, s.m. & adv. The very middle, the very midst; — in the very midst (of); in the exact middle or centre
s.m. Interposition, intervention, mediation, intercession; arbitration, settlement, reconciliation ( = bić-bićāʼo)
(-meṅ), To interpose, mediate; to intercede; to arbitrate, settle, adjust, reconcile, pacify
s.f. Parallel lines (syn. samānāntar rekhā; ḵẖut̤ūt̤-i-mutawāzī)
s.f. Middle language or dialect; mixture of Urdū and Hindī
v.n. To differ; to raise a quarrel between
bīć-meṅ paṛnā, v.n. To interpose, intervene, interfere; to negotiate (in a bargain, marriage, &c.); to be surety or security (for)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Situated in the middle or between; — the middle one, &c. ( = bićauliyā, q.v.)
ex: Put all the stuff in the middle.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀘𑁆𑀘 (vicca), from Sanskrit वीच्य (vīcya). Cognate Punjabi وِچّ (vicc).

def: s.m. Mask, visor.
more: n. m. A visor; a mask. Āge hī shakl ḍarānī hai terī bīchā sī, Tis pe yūṅ phāṛ-ke dīde na mujhe ghūr, dadā . Wom. Rangīn.

def: = bhićak, q.v.
v.n. To be alarmed, take fright ( = bijhaknā and bhićaknā); to run away, retreat, slink off; to recede or withdraw (from), back out (of), shrink (from = bićalnā, q.v.); to be sprained.
more: v. n. To be disappointed, to be balked: to sprain: to run away, to retreat: to withdraw a consent which has been given.
(بيچ Within, and کان Ear) s. m. An ornament worn in the anteriour lobe of the ear.
v. n. (dakh.) To fear, to be afraid ( Harris ).

def: s.f. Straw (cf. bićhānā, 'to spread'; bićhaunā, 'bedding').
more: n. f. H. bichhānā to spread. Straw (for the bedding of horses, etc.).

def: v.n. To move about, move to and fro; to roam, wander, ramble; to slip, shift, move out of place or away; to go astray, lose (one's) way; to be lost or missing; to be left behind; to be separated, parted, cut off ( = bićhaṛnā, q.v.); to retreat, draw back; to withdraw or back out (or a promise, &c.); to get out of order, be deranged, spoiled, or marred; to get out of harmony or tune; to get out of temper, become troublesome (as a disappointed child): — bićal-jānā = bićalnā.
more: v. n. S. वि + चल् to move about. 1. To slip; miss one's footing. Hāth bichal gayā, kām bigaṛ gayā . 2. To wander; roam; range; ramble. Kaṭhin bhūm, komal-pad-gāmī, Kaun het ban bichrahu, Swāmī? Rāmāyan. Hard is the ground, and lotus-soft thy feet, Why wander, Lord, in forests so unmeet? 3. To be separated, parted, cut off. 4. To go astray; lose one's way; to be lost or missing ( bichhaṛnā ). 1. Laṛkā mele meṅ se bichal gayā . 2. Sādh santh kā maram na jāne, bahu abhimān saṛe, Bichali jaīheṅ Jam ke duāre, agan kunḍ meṅ jare . Kabīr. Deep truths of perfect holy men unlearnt in pride they rot, At Hell's gates doomed to slip and burn in caldrons is their lot. 5. To retreat or withdraw from one's word or promise ( bāt se phirnā ). Dekho bhaī, kah-ke mat bichlo . 6. To get out of tune; fail. Gāte gāte bichat gayā . 7. To be spoiled, marred. Kām bichal gayä . 8. To turn mad. Dil bichlā burā . 9…
v. n. To turn, to bend, to slip, to break oneʼs promise, to infringe.

def: s.m. Consideration, reflection, meditation, deliberation, investigation, examination, trial, disputation, discussion, dispute; exercise of judgment or reason, judgment, discrimination, prudence; decision, determination; opinion, thought, apprehension, will
more: n. m. S. विचार from वि, चर to go. 1. Reflection ( soch ); thought; notion; idea. 2. Consideration; mediation; comtemplation; deliberation. Sab biddyā kar sār jag uttam ek bichār, Abichāre, budhvān kaheṅ, keval biddyā bhār . Dohā. Behārī Lāl. Of all the learning in the world the essence fine is thought, The void of thought, the wisemen say, are but the dross, else nought. 3. Conception; imagination; fancy; speculation. 4. Understanding; comprehension; sense; reason. 5. Discrimination; judgment; nice perception ( tamīz ). 6. Calculation; computation; reckoning. 7. Estimate; valuation; assessment. 8. Opinion; decision; verdict; finding ( tajwīz ).
s. m. Consideration, reflection, contrivance, judgment, opinion, thought, apprehension, will.

def: adj. & s.m. = bićārā, be-ćāra, q.q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Helpless, forlorn; — helpless person; poor creature, poor wretch (used in pity).
more: (for بي چاره q. v.) adj. Helpless.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Helpless, forlorn; — helpless person; poor creature, poor wretch (used in pity).
adj. & s.m. Deliberating; investigating; discussing; judging; — investigator, discriminator, inspector, judge.
more: see بچار) part. act. Investigator, judge, inspector.

def: part, Reflecting, thinking.
s.m. = bićārak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Deliberating; investigating; discussing; judging; — investigator, discriminator, inspector, judge.
part. Thought upon, deliberated upon, considered, meditated; decided, determined; &c.
adv. Upon or after due consideration or investigation, &c.; thoughtfully, deliberately, considerately, &c.

ety: bićār˚ = Prk. विचारे(इ) or विचार(इ) = S. विचारय(ति), rt. वि+चर्
def: v.n. and t. To think, deem, opine, conceive, apprehend; to consider, reflect, ponder, deliberate; to comprehend; to examine, discriminate, judge, investigate, estimate, calculate; to think of, resolve on, determine, decide, pronounce judgment on; to feel (pity, fear, &c.).
more: v. n. 1. To devote the mind, thoughts, or attention to. 2. To think; reflect; ponder; revolve. Log haṅseṅ, charchā kareṅ, man meṅ kareṅ bichār . Song. You'll be the talk, and folks will laugh and evil of you think. 3. To consider; contemplate; deliberate; pore over. 4. To fancy; surmise; guess; conjecture. 5. To distinguish; discriminate; discern; judge. 6. To examine; scrutinize; sift; inspect closely. 7. To estimate; value; fix ( āṅknā ). 8. To calculate; compute; reckon ( ginnā ). Bamnā shugan bichār, Hāth kā dūṅgī kāṅgnā, aur gal kā dūṅgī hār . Song. Ṭhumrī . An omen give me, Brahman, when my spouse I'll see again, My golden bracelets you shall have, and also my gold chain. 9. To think or speak ill of; depreciate; backbite. Hin. Wom. Pīṭ pīchhe kyoṅ kisī kā bichār kare hai? Hin. Wom.
v. a. To consider, reflect, think, investigate, comprehend, apprehend, conceive, to judge.

def: v.t. = bićalnā, and bićhaṛnā, q.q.v. v.n. To move about, move to and fro; to roam, wander, ramble; to slip, shift, move out of place or away; to go astray, lose (one's) way; to be lost or missing; to be left behind; to be separated, parted, cut off ( = bićhaṛnā, q.v.); to retreat, draw back; to withdraw or back out (or a promise, &c.); to get out of order, be deranged, spoiled, or marred; to get out of harmony or tune; to get out of temper, become troublesome (as a disappointed child): — bićal-jānā = bićalnā.
more: v. a. To separate, detail. بچربچرکربولنا bichar bichar kar bolnā, To speak distinctly.

def: s.m. = bićārak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Deliberating; investigating; discussing; judging; — investigator, discriminator, inspector, judge.
part. adj. To be deliberated, weighed or discussed; requiring deliberation, &c.; fit and proper to be discussed.
s.m. One who interposes; one who interferes or intermeddles; interposer; intermeddler; go-between; middle-man, ambassador, agent, broker; matchmaker (syn. bīć-wālā, tisrait).
more: s. m. An arbitrator, &c. See بچواني bich-wānī.

def: s.m. = bīć-bićāʼo, q.v.s.v. bīć.
s.m. Intermediate space or distance, interval; interim; difference (syn. antar, fāṣila; farq).
s.f. Scorpion = biććhū, q.v. s.m. Scorpion; the sign Scorpio of the zodiac; a kind of firework (also written biććhī).
s.m. Scorpion; the sign Scorpio of the zodiac; a kind of firework (also written biććhī).
more: n. m. S. वृश्चिक, Pr. vinchhuo . A scorpion. Also a plant. Sāṅp kā kāṭhā sove, bichchhū kā kāṭh ā rove . Prov. The snake-bitten sleep, the scorpion-bitten shriek.
s. m. A scorpion, the sign Scorpio of the Zodiack.
s. m. Name of a firework ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.m. = biććhū, q.v.; — and = bis, bish, bikh, q.q.v. s.m. Scorpion; the sign Scorpio of the zodiac; a kind of firework (also written biććhī).
s.m. = biććhū, q.v. s.m. Scorpion; the sign Scorpio of the zodiac; a kind of firework (also written biććhī).

s. Very middle.
adj. (f. -ī), Slippery.
v.n. = bićhaṛnā, q.v.
more: v. n. To be separated, to separate, to slip, slide, slip out of place, sprain, turn away the face.

def: v.t. To spread, spread out, extend, lay on; to make (a bed); to strew, scatter; to lay prostrate or flat, knock down, fell, lay low ( = bićhā-denā, the more common form in these senses).
more: v. a. S. बिछाना from विस्तर to spread. 1. To spread; lay on; floor; pave. 2. ( bichhaunā k .) To make the bed. 3. To strew; scatter; disperse; diffuse. Sāre ghar meṅ chāṅwal bichhā dīye . 4. ( bichhaunā k .) To knock down. Phr. To make one measure his length on the ground. Mārte mārte bichhā dīyā .
(trans. of بچهنا q. v.) v. a. To spread, extend.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀯𑀺𑀘𑁆𑀙𑀸𑀤𑁂𑀤𑀺 (*vicchādedi), from Sanskrit विच्छादयति (vicchādayati, “uncovers, unclothes”).

def: s.m. Carpet, bed, &c. = bićhhaunā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀯𑀺𑀘𑁆𑀙𑀸𑀤𑁂𑀤𑀺 (*vicchādedi), from Sanskrit विच्छादयति (vicchādayati, “uncovers, unclothes”).

def: v.n. = bićhaṛnā, q.v.
more: E. v. n. S. वि and छुट् to cut. To be parted, separated. See بچلنا (2 to 5). 1. Sājan ye mat jāniyo, toh bichhṛat mohe chain, Āle ban kī lākṛī silgat hūṅ din rain . Dohā. Think not, my husband, I can rest from you away, The green wood of the forest burns all night and day. 2. Pattā ṭūṭā peṛ se, le gaī pavan uṛāë, Ab-ke bichhṛeṅ kab mileṅ, dūr paṛe haiṅ jāë . Dohā. As leaves torn from the tree are borne away by winds so fleet, So parted now, cast far away, say shall we ever- meet? 3. Khudā laṛne kī rāt de, bichhaṛne kā din na de . Prov. God grant howe'er we quarrel may, we ne'er of parting see the day. 4. Rahe sāth pivat aru khāt, Bichhṛe woh nā din nā rāt . Riddle. Ans. Shadow. Eating or drinking, with me ever, By night or day he leaves me never.

def: v.n. To be separated, parted, divided, &c.; to separate, part, go asunder; to be sprained; to turn away or aside (from); to slip, slide ( = bićalnā, q.v.).
more: ( वि and r. छुट Cut) v. n. To separate, to be separated.

def: s.m. The stinging nettle, Urtica interrupta (syn. bićhuṭī, bićhuʼā).
more: W. n. m. A stinging-nettle; Urtica interrupta .
s. m. A stinging nettle. (Urtica interrupta).

def: v.n. = bićhaṛnā, q.v.

n. m. 1. One who interposes; a mediator; umpire. 2. A middleman; arbitrator; agent; broker. 3. A go-between; a matchmaker. 4. One who interferes; an intermeddler; intruder. bīchā bīch ; Illit. bīchoṅ bīch, bīcham bīch , adv. The very middle or centre.
s.m. Bed, bedding, bed-clothes; carpeting, carpet; the carpet on which (Hindu) women sit and bewail the death of a person; (hence, among Hindūs), prescribed period of wailing or lamentation
more: n. m. H. bichhānā to spread. 1. Bedding; bed-clothes; mattress. 2. A carpet; matting; a carpet on which Hindu women sit for a certain time to deplore the death of a person and chant dirges. Hin. 3. The prescribed period for lamentation. Hin. bichhonā k . v. a. See بچهانا 2, 3, 4. bichhau'nī, bichhonī , n. f. Dim. of bichhonā , (1).
subs: To open or spread out bedding, &c.; to make (one's) bed; to lay a carpet, &c.
To make a bed; — to lay prostrate or flat, knock down, fell ( = bićhā-denā).

adv. See بيچ bīch.
(see بچهنا) v. n. To spread; (metonim.) to knock down, to drub.
(from بچهنا q. v.) s. m. Bedding, bed-clothes, bed, carpetting.
n. m. Distribution of an aggregate sum.
v. a. To card; separate the seed from the cotton ( oṭnā ).
v. a. Caus. of bichhnā q. v.

s.f. Ornament or ring worn on the toe ( = bićhuʼā, q.v.). s.m. Scorpion ( = biććhū, q.v.); — a kind of dagger; a small stiletto with a curved blade; an iron claw or hook; a ring worn on the (little or great) toe (these various significations all arise from the shape of the dagger, &c. resembling that of a scorpion's sting); — the hemp plant, Crotalaria juncea (syn. san); the seed-pod of the same plant; — the stinging nettle, Urtica heterophylla; also Urtica interrupta ( = bićhātā and bićhuṭī).
n. f. An earring.
v.n. To be spread, be laid out; to be floored; to become prostrate, lie flat; to humble oneself; to be brought or laid low.
more: v. n. S. विस्तर from स्तृ to spread. 1. To be spread or laid on; to be floored. 2. To prostrate oneself; fall on the ground; lick the dust. Khātir ke māre bichchhe jāte haiṅ . He is polite to the very ground. 3. To be ruined, reduced to poverty. Kharch ke māre bichchhe jāte haiṅ .
(perhaps from विस्तर r. स्तृ Spread) v. n. To be spread, to spread.

def: v.t. To detach, disconnect, separate, part, divide, sunder; to pull or pick to pieces; to card (cotton).
more: (trans. of بچهڙنا) v. a. To separate.

def: s.m. = bićhohā, q.v. s.m. Separation, parting; absence.
more: n. m. H. bichhaṛnā to be separated. Parting ( bijog ); separation; absence. 1. Chakwā chakwī do jane, in mat māro koë, Yeh māre Kartār ke, rain bichhoā hoë . Dohā. Two swans there are, may cruel darts on them ne'er light, Stricken of Heaven the stream divides them all the night. 2. Sājan dukhyā kar gaë, sukh ko le gaë sāth, Janam bichhoyā de gaë, ghaṛī na baiṭhe pās . Dohā. My husband with him pleasure took and left me pain in dower, A life of separation—he ne'er sat with me an hour. 3. Mintr bichhoyā kaṭhin hai, mat dījo, Kartār, Sudh āwe jab mintr kī barseṅ nain aṅgār . Dohā. The absence of a friend, O God, ne'er grant so hard a lot! When thinking of an absent friend the tears flow burning hot.

s.m. = bićauliyā, q.v. s.m. One who interposes; one who interferes or intermeddles; interposer; intermeddler; go-between; middle-man, ambassador, agent, broker; matchmaker (syn. bīć-wālā, tisrait).
more: s. m. A mediator, interposer, arbitrator, agent, ambassadour.

def: s.m. = bićwānī, &c. and bićauliyā, q.v. s.m. = bićauliyā, q.v. s.m. One who interposes; one who interferes or intermeddles; interposer; intermeddler; go-between; middle-man, ambassador, agent, broker; matchmaker (syn. bīć-wālā, tisrait).
s.f. A small hole or pit into which children in play endeavour to throw balls or marbles.
more: s. f. A little pit into which children in play endeavour to throw balls or marbles.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بد (bad). Unrelated to English bad.

def: s.f. = bīdh, q.v. s.f. A (Hindū) marriage ceremony in which turmeric, salt, &c. are ground on the seventh or the third day before the wedding.
more: n. f. The Hin. marriage ceremony in which turmeric, salt, etc. are ground on the seventh or third day before the wedding. Byāh meṅ bīdh kā lekhā! Prov.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit वैद्य (vaidya).

def: s.f. Permission to depart, dismission, taking leave; departure; farewell
subs: (-ko), To dismiss, permit (one) to depart; to bid farewell; to see (one) off
v.n. To take leave, say farewell, depart; to depart this life, to die.

def: s.m. Mound; cluster of mounds.

n. f. S. विदाय A. وداع permission to go away. 1. Leave to depart; dismissal; departure; dismissal of the bride from her home. (Hin. Marriage ceremony). 2. Farewell; adieu; good-bye. bidā kar'nā , v. n. 1. To dismiss; permit to depart; give one his congè or dismissal. Mehr gaī, muhabbat gaī, gayā mān aur pān, Huqqe se muṅh jhulas-ke bidā kīyā mehmān . Gone kindness, gone affection, even respect with pān is gone, His mouth burnt with the proffered pipe, the door the guest is shown. 2. To say or bid adieu; to bid farewell; to see one off. Ek sajan woh kharā piyārā, Jā se ghar morā bhayā ujyārā, Bhor bhaī tab bidā maīṅ kīyā, "E sakhī, sājan?" "Nā sakhī, dīyā." Riddle. Mukrī . A youth most dear and beauteous, His presence lighted up the house, We parted as the night took flight. "A lover dear?" "No, dear, the light." bidā honā , v. n. 1. To take leave; depart; say good bye. 2. To depart this life; to die. Sakal sabhā ko Rām Rām, bidā hot hai Gaṅg . Kabit.
more: s. f. Dismission, taking leave, farewell, adieu. بداکرنا bidā karnā, To bid farewell, to dismiss.

def: n. f. 1. Dismissal; departure. Ghoṛā chāhiye bidāëgī ko, "zarā phirtā sā āīyo ." Prov. The parting guest to speed the lucky moment's now. You want a horse—"please call again"—too late I trow. 2. Presents from the host on the departure of the servant.
s.f. Presents made by a host to a visitor, or to a servant, on dismissing him; dismissal; departure ( = bidā).
v.t. To turn the plough over a field after the seed is come up; to plough immediately after sowing for the purpose of covering the seed; to harrow.
more: v. a. To turn the plough over a field after the seed is come up; to plough immediately after sowing for the purpose of covering the seed.
v. a. 1. To turn the plough over a field after the seed is come up, to plough immediately after sowing for the purpose of covering the seed. 2. To harrow.

def: s.f. Presents made by a host to a visitor, or to a servant, on dismissing him; dismissal; departure ( = bidā).
v.n. To be scared; to start, shy, &c. ( = bijhaknā, q.v.); — to move, stir; to move aside or out of the way; to shift; to draw back, shrink (from); to be dissuaded.
more: v. n. 1. To start; shy ( chauṅknā ). 2. To be scared; to take flight. 3. To be dissuaded ( uchaṭnā , 4. 5.).

def: s.m. A kind of harrow (worked by oxen to loosen the soil and remove weeds from the standing crops of young rice).
more: n. m. S. वीज seed, दृ to tear up. A rake or harrow worked by oxen to loosen the soil and extricate weeds from among the standing crops of young rice.

def: s.m. A vessel of mixed metal (particularly bharat, q.v.) plated with silver by beating; plating metal with silver by beating (the name is said to be derived from the town of Bidar in the Dakkhan)
subs: s.m. A manufacturer of vessels described above, one who plates metal (especially bharat) with silver.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بدر (ba-dar), from به (ba) + در (dar).

def: s.m. Tearing, rending; breaking, cleaving, splitting, severing, dividing.
more: s. m. Tearing, rending, breaking, cleaving, splitting, severing, dividing.

def: v.t. To tear, rend, &c. (see bidāran).
more: v. a. S. ट्ट, P. darīdan to tear. To tear; rend.
Rus. v. a. To exclude; drive away ( nikālnā ); turn out; dismiss. Bin hile ko hilāīye nahīṅ, aur hile ko biḍārye nahīṅ . Prov. Pet not the wild, nor drive away the tame.
v. a. To tear, to rend.

ety: biḍār˚ = Prk. विडारे(इ) or विडार(इ) = S. विदारय(ति), caus. of rt. वि+दॄ
def: v.t. To drive away, turn away, turn out, expel, exclude; to efface.
more: v. a. 1. To drive away, to turn away. 2. To efface.

ety: biḍar˚ = Prk. विडर(इ) = S. विदृणा(ति), rt. वि+ दॄ; — cf. ḍālnā
def: v.n. = bitharnā; and v.t. = bithrānā; q.q.v. v.t. To spread, scatter, &c. ( = bithrānā, q.v.; and see also bitharnā, of which it is the caus.).
s.f. Novelty or innovation in religion, schism, heresy; wrong-doing, wrong, violence, oppression, outrage; strife, contention, quarrel
more: s. f. The introduction of novelty or change in religion, schism, heresy, innovation; violence, oppression, wrong.
subs: (-par), To do violence or wrong (to); to practise oppression (on); to contend, quarrel.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بِدْعَت (bid'at).

def: s.m. Innovator in religion; heretic, schismatic; wrong-doer; oppressor, tyrant; contentious or quarrelsome person.
more: n. m. 1. An innovator in religion; a heretic. 2. A quarrelsome or disputatious person; a wrangler. Illit. 3. A tyrant; oppressor. Illit.

def: s.f. Presents made by a host to a visitor, or to a servant, on dismissing him; dismissal; departure ( = bidā).
s.m. Another country, foreign land; — adv. To another country, abroad (syn. pardes).
more: n. m. S. विदेश from वि second देशः country, Pr. वईदेसो foreign. Abroad; a foreign country; another country; a foreign land ( par-des ). 1. Mere piyā gaë bades, moë chūnar kaun raṅgā de? Song. Malhār. My true love gone to a far country, a bright gay skirt who'll dye for me? 2. Ṭuk hirs o havā ko chhoṛ, miyāṅ, mat des bades phire mārā . Nazīr. Thy greed, Sir, just a wee repress, nor roam from place to place bootless. 3. Jo nar jāt bides ko sāmāṅ karat anant, Nā jānūṅ par-lok ko kaise nar nishchant . Dohā.
s. m. A foreign country, another country.

def: adj. & s.m. Of another country, from foreign parts; foreign; wretched; — foreigner; traveller; outsider, stranger.
more: adj. Of another country; from foreign parts.
n. m. S. विदेशी A foreigner See اوپِري (1).

def: s.f. A (Hindū) marriage ceremony in which turmeric, salt, &c. are ground on the seventh or the third day before the wedding.
s.m. Determination of the amount to be paid as government revenue, settlement (syn. band-o-bast).
s.m. Sending; ordering; arrangement, disposition; creation; ordinance, injunction, &c. ( = bidhi, q.v.); worship, ceremony; gaining, gain; wealth; act of hostility; conflict of opposite feelings.
more: s. m. Common practice, precept, direction. See بده विधि sign 1. and 2.

def: s.m. Arranger, disposer, maker, creator; providence; name of Brahmā (as creator of the world); — fate, destiny (syn. bidhnā).
more: n. m. S. विधाता. 1. God. 2. Destiny. Log, kuṭamb, barjeṅ sab mo ko, nīkī na lāgī kāhū kī bātā, Mere to chitr meṅ mittr base hai, mittr ke chitr kī jāne Bidhātā . Kabit. My housefolk bid me love him not, their words I do not love, His image in my heart is fixed, his heart knows God above.

def: s.m. = bidhātā, q.v. s.m. Arranger, disposer, maker, creator; providence; name of Brahmā (as creator of the world); — fate, destiny (syn. bidhnā).
s.f. Widow.
more: n. f. S. विधवा from वि without, धव husband. A widow ( rāṅḍ ).
s. m. The deity. See بدهاتا.

def: s.f. Piercing, perforation.
v.t. To reduce, lessen, diminish, abate.

s.f. Rule, form, formula, sacred precept; precept, maxim; injunction, order, command; law, statute, ordinance; direction (for the performance of a rite), sacred text or scripture prescribing any act or observance; any prescribed act, rite, or ceremony; act, action; behaviour, conduct, mode of life; method, manner, mode, way, means; kind, sort; fate, destiny, lot; — balance of an account; adjustment of an account; — s.m. Name of the god Brahmā; of the god Vishnu
subs: bidh milnā, v.n. To agree, correspond, tally; an agreement to be come to; bargain to be struck

To consult sacred texts with respect to performing a rite or ceremony (as a marriage, &c.); to consult the horoscope of the bride and bridegroom before performing the marriage ceremony; — to check or clear an account; to strike a balance
adj. According to rule, law, precept, &c.

def: v.n. To be pierced or perforated (see bedhnā); to be wounded.
more: n. m. 1. Brahma; the Supreme Being. Sajan sakāre jāëṅge, nain mareṅge roë, Bidhnā, aisī rain kar, bhor kabhū nā hoë . Dohā. My husband hence tomorrow goes, tears will wear out my eyes. O God! on such a night as this may morning never rise! 2. Destiny; fate; doom ( beh-mātā ).

def: s.m. Arranger, disposer, &c. = bidhātā, q.v. s.m. Arranger, disposer, maker, creator; providence; name of Brahmā (as creator of the world); — fate, destiny (syn. bidhnā).
s.m. The moon; — name of Brahmā; name of Vishṇu.
s.m. Falling to ruin, ruin, destruction, annihilation, extermination.
part. adj. Known, familiar; famed, famous, renowned.
v.t. To scare; to startle, &c. ( = bijhkānā, q.v.); to cause to recede or draw back; to dissuade; to alienate, estrange.
more: v. a. S. भयचकित alarmed. 1. To alienate; estrange. See uchṭānā (4.) 2. To startle; frighten; cause to shy.

def: v. a. 1. To screw. 2. To mock at, to laugh at, to ridicule.
To open out, lay open; to expose; to screw; to expose to ridicule; to laugh at, mock, ridicule.
more: E. v. a. S. विदारण from हृ to tear. To open out; lay open; expand. dāṅt bidoṛ'nā , E. v. a. To shew one's teeth; make faces at ( mūṅh banānā ); mock; play the monkey.

def: To open out, lay open; to expose; to screw; to expose to ridicule; to laugh at, mock, ridicule.
more: v. a. To laugh at. See بدورنا.

def: s.f. Mixed metal (composed of copper, zinc, and tin) plated with silver (see bidar: It is used to make cups and other vessels, and ḥuqqā bottoms); mixed metal (syn. bharat).
more: n. f. A mixed metal of copper, zinc, and tin, of which vessels and huqqā bottoms are made, so called from being made originally at Bidar in the Dakkan.
s. f. A kind of tutanag inlaid with silver, used to make ḥuḳḳa bottoms, cups, &c. and, so called from Beeder, the name of a city and province.

def: prep. Without, deprived of; with the exception of, besides.
adj. Learned, instructed, intelligent, wise, scientific.

s.f. Learning, science; knowledge; intellect; skill, art; a magical pill or bolus (which placed in the mouth is supposed to give the power of ascending to heaven); magical knowledge or skill; — name of a small insect (called also Saraswatī)
more: n. f. S. विद्या , Pr. vijjā , from विद् to know. 1. Learning; book-learning; lore; science. ☞ It is of fourteen kinds—the four Veds, the six auxiliary sciences ( aṅg ) for the right understanding of the Veds, as Grammar, Prosody, Astronomy, etc., with the Purāns, the Mīṅmāsā or Theology, Nyāy or Logic, Dharam or law. 1. Khetī kareṅ na banje jāëṅ, Biddyā ke bal baiṭhe khāëṅ. Biddyā astutī . (The Indian school master's precept). Nor work in fields, nor counting house you need, By dint of learning sit at home and feed. 2. Bin biddyā ke nar aur nār, jaise hove gadhā kumhār . Prov. Void of learning men and women both, just like the potter's donkey are in sooth. 3. Biddyā sam koū dhan nahīṅ eh jag dūjā aur, Ghaṭe na dīye amol jo, le na sake jahīṅ chor . Dohā. Behārī Lāl. In all the world like learning priceless there's no wealth, Parted with 'tis no less, it cant be taken by stealth. 2. Practi…
subs: s.m. A particular kind of good or evil genius attending upon the gods; — a learned man; one skilled in magical knowledge
(vidyā+arthī), s.m. Seeker after knowledge, student, scholar, pupil (syn. t̤ālib-ʻilm)
s.m. Abode of learning, school, college.

def: s. m. A sort of demi-god or attendant in the celestial courts. (The Vidyādharas dance before the assembled deities in Indraʼs heaven).
n. m. A seeker after knowledge; student ( chaṭṭā ); pupil; learner; scholar; bookworm.
adj. & s.m. Learned, scientific, skilled; a learned man, a scholar ( = bidwān, vidwān).
more: adj. Learned; versed in. Paṛhe na likkhe, nām Biddyā-dhar . Prov. chaudah biddyā nidhān , adj. nidhān a receptacle. Versed in the fourteen sciences. 1. A master of practical sciences; jack of all trades. 2. ( Ironic .) An ignorant man; a fool; blockhead. Āp to chaudhā biddyā nidhān haiṅ .

def: adj. (dakh.) Crooked, bent: see بيکا bīkā under s. بال Hair, and see بنگا bingā.
s.m. = bīghā, q.v.
s.f. Rent fixed on lands per bīghā; — adv. per bīghā ( = bighoṭī; bīghoṭī, q.v.).
adj. Strange, &c. = be-gāna, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Strange, foreign, another, not related, not domestic, not an acquaintance or friend, alien, unknown; — stranger, foreigner
subs: adj. Of an unfriendly, or savage disposition

def: adj. Strange, &c. = be-gāna, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Strange, foreign, another, not related, not domestic, not an acquaintance or friend, alien, unknown; — stranger, foreigner
subs: adj. Of an unfriendly, or savage disposition

def: s.f. Strangeness, &c. = be-gānagī, q.v. s.f. Strangeness, the being foreign or not domestic; estrangement; shyness.
s.f. Bad smell, stench, stink, fetor (syn. bad-bo).
s. f. Stink, fetor.
s.m. = begār, q.v. s.m. Compelling to work for nothing; compulsory labour with or without pay; impressment of workmen, carriage, &c.; — a person forced to labour ( = be-gārī)
more: n. m. S. विघट्ट to break asunder. 1. Impairment; perversion; deterioration; wane; corruption; vitiation; degeneration; debasement. Bigāṛ saṅvār Khudā ke hāth . Prov. To make and mar in God's hands are. 2. Disorganization; decline; decay. Ālamgīr ke waqt se saltanat meṅ bigāṛ paṛā . 3. Disfigurement; defacement; mutilation. 4. Disorder; disarrangement; disturbance; confusion. 5. Disturbance; interruption; hindrance; stop. 6. Mismanagement; maladministration; misrule. 7. Downfall; ruin; wreck; overthrow; subversion. Mulk ke bigāṛ kī yehī wajah thī . 8. Loss; waste; detriment. (Rare). Pataṅg-bāzī meṅ rupaë kā bigāṛ hai . 9. Injury; damage; loss; harm. Is kām ke karne meṅ, tumhārā kyā bigāṛ hai .? 10. Blunder; mistake; fault. 11. Failing; short-coming; defect; vice ( burāī ). Taqdīr ke bigāṛ kī tadbīr kyā kareṅ, Bantī nahīṅ hai koī bhī tadbīr, yā nasīb! Zafar. 12. Disease; sickness; disorder…
subs: v.t. To press into service with or without pay
v.n. To work in a half-hearted or perfunctory manner; to work carelessly.

def: s.m. Change for the worse, impairment, deterioration, corruption, vitiation, disfigurement, defacement; decline, decay; disorder, disarrangement, disturbance, confusion; ruin, violation, break, smash; harm, injury, damage; defect, blemish, stain, flaw; difference, disagreement, discord, misunderstanding, quarrel; defection, rebellion, revolt, mutiny
more: (from بگاڙنا) s. m. Violation, difference, quarrelling, discord, misunderstanding; damage, injury. بگاڙکرنا bigāṛ karnā, v. a. To quarrel, to forfeit friendship. بگاڙپرآنا bigāṛ par ānā, v. n. To be ready to quarrel.
subs: v.n. To be ready to quarrel; — bigāṛ karnā (-kā), To harm, spoil; to quarrel; to forfeit friendship.

ety: bigāṛ˚ = Prk. विगाढे(इ) or विगाढ(इ) = S. विघाटय(ति), caus. of वि+घट्
def: v.t. To ruin, spoil, mar, vitiate, &c. (see bigāṛ); to bungle; to break a custom; to cause a misunderstanding (between friends); to foul (one's clothes)
more: (trans. of بگڙنا) v. a. 1. To spoil, mar, damage, injure, ruin, bungle. 2. To cause misunderstanding between friends.

ety: bigaṛ˚ = Prk. विगढ(इ) = S. विघट(ते), rt. वि+घट्
def: v.n. To be changed for the worse, to become worse; to be impaired, deteriorated, defaced, disfigured, distorted; to take harm, be damaged, injured, marred, spoiled, corrupted, vitiated, ruined, destroyed; to fall off; to fail, miscarry; to break down; to go or turn bad; to get out of order, be disordered, tossed, disarranged; to be mismanaged, bungled; to be wasted, squandered; to disagree, be at variance, be estranged, to quarrel; to get out of temper, become angry or enraged; to become vicious, wicked, or unruly; to rebel, revolt, mutiny
more: v. n. To be spoiled, damaged or marred, to fail of success, miscarry, to quarrel, to disagree, to be at variance, to be enraged.

def: n. m. 1. A spendthrift ( uṛāū ). 2. A spoiler; destroyer.
s.m. Ruiner, spoiler, destroyer.
v.n. = bikasnā, q.v. v.n. To open, expand; to blow (as a flower), bloom; to be pleased or delighted, become radiant with joy; to smile, laugh
subs: v.n. To be blighted, to fade, droop; to be displeased.

def: s.f. Bad conduct, misdemeanour; badness.
more: ( विगती i. e. वि and गति Conduct) s. f. Badness.

def: n. f. 1. Land measurement. 2. Rate per bīghā ; rent of a bīghā .
s.m. = bīghā, q.v.
s.m. A land-measure equal to about five-eighths of an acre. (The kaććā bīghā varies from a fourth to a third of an acre)
more: n. m. S. बिगह. A measure of land. A pakkā bīghā is equal to 5/8 of an acre. A kachchā bīghā is from a third to a fourth of an acre. fī bīghā, bīghā-dām, bīghā-sar, bīghe gail, bīgah-wār . Rate per bīghā .
s. m. A quantity of land containing twenty kaṭṭhās or 120 feet square.
subs: bīghā-sar, bīghā-wār, bīghe-gail, Rate per bighā.

def: for بيگها bīghā, q. v.
s.m. Obstacle, impediment, hindrance, obstruction, stop, prevention, interruption, opposition; any difficulty or trouble
more: n. m. S. विघ्न from वि, हन् kill. 1. Bloodshed; slaughter; carnage ( qatl-i-ām ). 2. Interruption; hindrance. bighan ḍāl'nā, karnā, yā machānā , v. a. 1. To kill; slaughter; butcher; spreadhavoc. 2. To interfere with; interrupt; confound.
s. m. Hinderance, stop, prevention, interruption.
subs: bighan karnā (-meṅ), To throw obstacles (in the way), to obstruct, hinder, interrupt, &c.

ety: S. विघन; rt. हन् with वि
def: s.m. Killing, slaying; slaughter, havoc
subs: bighan maćānā, v.t. To kill, slay, slaughter, make havoc.

def: s.m. Wolf ( = bik).
v.n. = bigaṛnā, q.v. v.t. To ruin, spoil, mar, vitiate, &c. (see bigāṛ); to bungle; to break a custom; to cause a misunderstanding (between friends); to foul (one's clothes)
s.f. Land measurement; rate per bīghā; — adv. Per bīghā (see bigahṭī).
s.f. & adv. = bigahṭī, q.v. s.f. Rent fixed on lands per bīghā; — adv. per bīghā ( = bighoṭī; bīghoṭī, q.v.).
v. a. To throw (pheṅknā) .

ety: S. विगानीयं, rt. गै with वि
def: v.t. To decry, speak ill of, blame, reproach, villify, ridicule; to ruin, destroy.
more: Wom. v. a. H. bī against, kah say. See بدگوی E jī, yeh bahū to mujhe rāt din bigove hai . Hin. Wom. My dear madam, this daughter-in-law of mine all day and night speaks ill of me.

def: part. adj. (f. bigoʼī), Decried, blamed, &c.; — s.f. Detraction, evil-speaking, scandal; ridicule, satire.
more: n. m. 1. Evil-speaking 2. A lampoon; satire ( haju ).

def: part. (of bigaṛnā) and adj. (f. -ī), Impaired, damaged, spoiled; out of temper, angry, &c. (see bigaṛnā); — bigṛe dil, adj. Rebellious, refractory, mutinous; reckless, wild, desperate; mad, crazy.
more: adj. (dakh.) Not cleared of the husk (seed of sesamum).

def: s.m. War, battle, quarrel, strife.
v.t. = bigāṛnā: and bikhernā; q.q.v. v.t. To ruin, spoil, mar, vitiate, &c. (see bigāṛ); to bungle; to break a custom; to cause a misunderstanding (between friends); to foul (one's clothes)
s.m. = bigāṛ, q.v. s.m. = begār, q.v. s.m. Compelling to work for nothing; compulsory labour with or without pay; impressment of workmen, carriage, &c.; — a person forced to labour ( = be-gārī)
subs: v.t. To press into service with or without pay
v.n. To work in a half-hearted or perfunctory manner; to work carelessly.

ety: S. विकृति; or v.n. fr. bigaṛnā
def: s.f. Damage, harm; spoil; misunderstanding (between friends), disagreement; war, battle (cf. bigār).
more: (from بگڙنا) s. f. Spoil, damage; misunderstanding between friends, war, battle.

def: s.m. One of the rāgs or musical modes.
n. m. A musical mode ( rāgnī ).
more: s. m. Name of a rāginī.

def: s.m. = bihāg, q.v. s.m. One of the rāgs or musical modes.

s.f. A spirit, supposed to tease infants by whispering alternately sad and pleasing things in their ears (which is the cause of their laughing and crying when asleep or awake); — a song of congratulation, &c. ( = badhāwā, q.v.).
wety: From بہاء (“Baháʼ”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s. f. A spirit, supposed to tease infants by whispering alternately sad and pleasing things in their ears, which is the cause of their laughing and crying when asleep or awake.

Hin. Wom. n. f. 1. A spirit supposed to whisper alternately sad and pleasing things in the ears of infants and so cause them to laugh and cry while asleep or awake. 2. A song of congratulation sung at the birth of a child. Pahle bihāī gā lo . Wom.
s.m. Dawn, day-light, day-break, morning, morrow (syn. bhinsār)
subs: adv. At dawn, at day-break, in the morning, early, soon, betimes.

def: n. m. Morning; dawn; break of day ( taṛkā ).
